rulings.Case Title: RICCI CORNELL VS. SUNG KWONG HUEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of 3rd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 30, 2015, line 7. Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of 3rd Amended Complaint DENIED. Plaintiff adequately pleads punitive damages claims. Whether the claims can be proven is a matter for another day. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: RICCI CORNELL VS. SUNG KWONG HUEY ET AL

rulings.Case Title: 1049 MARKET STREET LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CHAD POTTER ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 30, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA BESELER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default By Clerk GRANTED, no opposition filed. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO TAY VS. HENRY PAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 30, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT'S HENRY PAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED; defendant has 10 days from notice of entry of order to answer the complaint. Defendant repeatedly disputes pleaded facts, which is not the function of a demurrer. Further, defendant insists on a level of detail in the complaint that is not required in California, which is a notice-pleading jurisdiction. Nor does defendant cite authority for his notion that a "complete copy of the lease" had to be attached to the complaint. Finally, a general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FELLOWSHIP MANOR APARTMENTS VS. ALBERT BRELAND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant Albert Breland
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 30, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANT ALBERT BRELAND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant Albert Breland GRANTED. According to the Proof of Service on file this motion was personally served on October 22, 2015, proving the required 5 day notice. There is no triable issue of fact regarding the required length of the notice of termination. Paragraph 9(b)(2) provides for the notice in accordance with the State law and HUD Regulation. Pursuant to 24 CFR 247.4(c) the length of the notice is determined by the rental agreement and state law. Rental agreement provides for a 30-day notice or state law, whichever is later. Lease, paragraph 9(b)(1). Therefore, pursuant to CFR 247.4(c) and rental agreement, 10-day notice of termination is insufficient and cannot support this unlawful detainer action. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FREDDIE R NEW VS. BILL NELLSON
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 30, 2015, line 5. DEFENDANT WILLIAM NELSON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that the tenancy is not subject to the Rent Ordinance. If tenancy is subject to SFRO, the notices are fatally defective. = (501/RBU)
